verses.fhb_text: "I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Predicate "I am" (vine metaphor) stays lowercase; Jesus names his own Person-relation to the Father.

Renderings:
• "I am the true vine" — predicate egō eimi + metaphor → lowercase per FHB-1.2-6
• "my Father" — Jesus's unique filial relation, μου retained per FHB-4.0.3-1
• "gardener" — geōrgos, modern English over archaic "vinedresser" per FHB-2.5-1

verses.fhb_text: Every branch in me that bears no fruit he cuts away, and every branch that does bear fruit he prunes, so that it will bear more fruit.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father (established v.1) carried by pronoun "he" per pronoun-shift rule.

Renderings:
• "cuts away" / "prunes" — vivid verbs for airei / kathairei per FHB-7.8-1

verses.fhb_text: You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Direct address to disciples; reads naturally in modern English.

verses.fhb_text: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it remains in the vine, neither can you unless you remain in me.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: menō rendered "remain" consistently for the chapter's key verb.

verses.fhb_text: I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever remains in me, and I in them, bears much fruit; for apart from me you can do nothing.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Predicate "I am the vine" lowercase per FHB-1.2-6.

Renderings:
• "apart from me you can do nothing" — chōris emou; definitive statement preserved

verses.fhb_text: If anyone does not remain in me, he is thrown out like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Judgment imagery preserved without softening.

Renderings:
• "thrown out" / "withers" / "burned" — specific verbs per FHB-7.8-1

verses.fhb_text: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: "will be done" — modern future for genēsetai per FHB-9.10-1.

verses.fhb_text: This is how my Father is glorified: that you bear much fruit and prove to be my disciples.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus names "my Father" — his unique relation.

Renderings:
• "my Father is glorified" — edoxasthē, Father as the one glorified per FHB-4.0.3-1

verses.fhb_text: As the Father has loved me, so have I loved you. Remain in my love.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father-Son love extended to the disciples — heart of the discourse.

Renderings:
• "the Father has loved me" — Jesus's relation; preserve "the Father" as he speaks it

verses.fhb_text: If you keep my commands, you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commands and remain in his love.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "my Father" then pronoun "his" per pronoun-shift.

Renderings:
• "my Father's commands" — Jesus's filial obedience per FHB-4.0.3-1

verses.fhb_text: I have told you these things so that my joy may be in you and your joy may be complete.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Joy-language preserved; reads naturally.

verses.fhb_text: This is my command: love one another as I have loved you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: The central command stated plainly.

verses.fhb_text: No one has greater love than this: to lay down one's life for one's friends.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Friendship-love theme — aligns with FHB's friendship-with-the-Father mission.

verses.fhb_text: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: "friends" (philoi) — the relational goal Jesus offers.

verses.fhb_text: I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know what his master is doing. Instead, I have called you friends, for everything I learned from my Father I have made known to you.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: From servants to friends — Jesus reveals the Father's heart, not just commands.

Renderings:
• "from my Father" — para tou patros mou, Jesus's relation per FHB-4.0.3-1
• "servants" / "master" — doulos / kyrios in the household sense; not divine Lord
verses.footnote: Jesus moves disciples from servants to friends by sharing everything he has heard from his Father — this is the Father's heart goal for every reader, friendship through the Son.

verses.fhb_text: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you to go and bear fruit—fruit that will last—so that whatever you ask the Father in my name he will give you.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Asking the Father in Jesus's name.

Renderings:
• "ask the Father in my name" — Jesus distinguishes Father from himself per FHB-4.0.3-1
• one em-dash pair around "fruit that will last" — appositional emphasis per FHB-9.5-1

verses.fhb_text: This is my command to you: love one another.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Command repeated as the hinge into the world-hatred section.

verses.fhb_text: "If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Transition to the world's hatred; preserve the sober tone.

verses.fhb_text: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. But you do not belong to the world; instead I have chosen you out of the world, and that is why the world hates you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Reads naturally in modern English; no FHB-distinctive choices.

verses.fhb_text: Remember what I told you: 'A servant is not greater than his master.' If they persecuted me, they will persecute you also. If they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "servant"/"master" in the household sense, not divine Lord.

Renderings:
• "master" — kyrios as the servant's earthly owner; not Father-language

verses.fhb_text: But they will treat you this way because of my name, for they do not know the one who sent me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "the one who sent me" is the Father — sending language per FHB-4.3.2-1.

Renderings:
• "the one who sent me" — ton pempsanta me; the Father distinguished as sender

verses.fhb_text: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Plain modern rendering; no distinctive choices.

verses.fhb_text: Whoever hates me hates my Father as well.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father-Son unity stated in reverse — to hate the Son is to hate the Father.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — Jesus's relation per FHB-4.0.3-1

verses.fhb_text: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have seen these works, and yet they have hated both me and my Father.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Works of the Son testify to the Father; rejection of both is one act.

Renderings:
• "both me and my Father" — kai eme kai ton patera mou per FHB-4.0.3-1

verses.fhb_text: But this happened to fulfill what is written in their Law: 'They hated me without cause.'
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Jesus cites Ps 35:19 / 69:4; the psalmist's words applied to himself.

Renderings:
• "They hated me without cause" — quotation rendered per FHB OT reading, applied to Jesus per FHB-1.2-23

verses.fhb_text: "When the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father—the Spirit of truth who goes out from the Father—he will testify about me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Paraclete — one Spirit named by relation to the Father and the Son.

Renderings:
• "the Helper" — paraklētos; Jesus's chosen title per FHB-1.2-7(F)
• "the Spirit of truth who goes out from the Father" — preserved per FHB-1.2-7
• "from the Father" — para tou patros; Father distinguished as sender per FHB-4.0.3-1
• one em-dash pair for the appositional clause per FHB-9.5-1
verses.footnote: The Helper Jesus promises is the Holy Spirit — sent by Jesus from the Father, named here "the Spirit of truth" because he leads disciples into all that Jesus has revealed.

verses.fhb_text: And you also must testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Disciples join the Spirit in testifying; reads naturally.

chapter_notes: John 15 is spoken entirely by Jesus to his disciples on the night before the cross. Per FHB-1.2-21 / FHB-4.0.3-1 grammatical agreement, Jesus's references to the Father use 'my Father' (his unique Person-relation) or 'the Father' as he speaks it — never collapsed to narrator-voice 'our Father.' Predicate 'I am' statements (vine, v.1, v.5) stay lowercase per FHB-1.2-6; there is no absolute 'I AM' in this chapter. The Paraclete (v.26) is rendered 'the Helper' with the 'Spirit of truth' title preserved per FHB-1.2-7; helper/Holy-Spirit footnote category already used in an earlier chapter, so no new footnote. All divine pronouns lowercase.
